reviews.review_msg: his lectures were so convoluted and abstract that it made it extremely hard to follow along. A lot of his points relied heavily on theoretical terms that honestly took attention away from the books. The texts he chose I did genuinely enjoy, but his form of teaching is genuinely so confusing

I would not recommend this course to anyone. The lectures are packed with random references/comments that seem unrelated to the course. It leaves you having no clue what information is relevant. It did not feel like a Canadian Literature class, but rather the instructor's personal political opinions.

I made the mistake of taking this class in my last semester. After four years of HONOURS English, I have never been more confused or infuriated by a Professor. I can say with certainty that Prof. Burnham should NOT be teaching this course.
Avoid this professor unless you want to spend an entire semester confused about what the hell you're supposed to be learning.
DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASSES.
the frankfurt school were composed of JUICCESS

He has a criteria for all discussion assignments that he doesn't and will not disclose. He words his questions and discussions to be very welcome of any and all ideas, but he actually has format, concepts, wording, or ideas he wants you to focus on that he never explicitly states. He confuses his students often with what they should be focusing on.

If you are not a graduate student I would not take a class with him. His concepts were so far beyond what anyone understand and assumes that people are absolute geniuses in abstract concepts. He does not give feedback for your work. I had absolutely zero idea what he was talking about because he uses words that no one understands.

This man somehow did it. He insightfully critiqued the connection between communism and s** and expected us to write about it in our essays. I let out a tear as I began writing about how the lines in a book siding up against each other were se*y for my grade. Avoid him..

The class consisted of Burnham bringing his communist pals to indoctrinate his students. No devices were allowed, and only handwritten notes were permitted; slides were never posted, and there were no criteria for the 10 assignments we wrote. Literally wears Jordans with a suit (who does that?). Can't imagine what he's like in upper-level courses.

Lectures and slides were not uploaded online, Clint is a yapper to the bone, and lectures were very fastpaced making his handwritten note taking rule super difficult. The final exam was way too much writing (11-13 pages in 2 hours) and very overwhelming.

Clint is a weird prof, I personally thinks if he wants to talk abt AI and what's going on today he shouldn't state that a protest sign saying “chat is this real” relates to AI when it's obvi just a saying… I averaged 60% on everything, seeked help ask how I could improve n changed and didn't notice a grade change until the finals.hes weird n old

Hate this guy
He's so weird, lowk obsessed with sex
Doesn't post lectures and banned all technology in lectures
I would never take a class with him everything was so boring and the Marxism book was SO boring and hard to understand, it was literally just a bunch of lectures in a book
Dude grades you on how communist and anti-capitalist you are.

He gives very interesting lectures. However, his concepts are hard to follow and his lecture materials often feel out of place for an English course. He gives no clear expectations for grading and this makes the writing assignments and grading of assignments feel arbitrary. Feedback is minimal and makes the course hard to succeed in.
He's terrible. Its a first-year class but he talks about concepts that most student do not know. It has no mandatory attendance but he won't post lecture slides. BEWARE

Clint is very knowledgable within his subject area, the content of the class this semester didn't bug me at all. I rated him low because of his teaching style, he goes fast and this was a first year course so some students can't quite keep up. He also doesn't post his slides so make sure you know people in the class in case you miss a lecture.

Don't even bother taking this class unless you want to do 3 essays in 2 hours for the final exam. This professor deliberately tries to make your life harder by giving you Marxist books to learn about things no one ever talks about. He also makes you read a  ridiculous poetry book that talks about oil and love.

I would rather take an actual Political Science or Communications course, why am I in an English course listening to Marxist theories. If the professor sees this review, please consider actually teaching English instead of teaching out of a Marxist theory book.

This course feels more like a Communications course or a Political Science course, despite not teaching anything about literature and only teaching his own interpretations, this is some how a required english course at SFU. Avoid this prof if you don't care for Marxist and Socialist ideologies and if you arnt willing to just play to his ego.
I wish he'd Derek Chauvin me so I wouldn't need to listen to his lectures anymore

Clint Burnham is as far left as you can get - seriously might be a communist btw. Anyways he keeps talking about sex & anti-capitalist sentiment while bringing in his communist pals, forcing students to buy their books (talk about irony right?). Overall this course is horrible, it forces students to "think" his way and if you don't you do poorly.

I thought I could be different and cool and go with the not-so-fun-sounding professor because the class seemed interesting. Holy moly dont do that. He is rude and demeaning. Stay away from his orange sneakers and horny ahh lisp.

Clint Burnham's ENGL115W is a perverse Marxist orgy, twisting lit into sex-laced commie propaganda. His lisp slurs every forced connection—pure agony. This isn't education; it's his twisted fantasy. Dodge this disaster!

Clint Burnham's ENGL115W? More like a communist kink seminar. He shoehorns sex into every Marxist rant, turning lit studies into his personal fetish hour. That lisp? Nails on a chalkboard. Total waste—skip this clown show!

Clint Burnham's ENGL115W is a Marxist sex-fest disguised as literature class. Every topic loops back to communism via his creepy obsession with sex. His lisp turns lectures into torture—insufferable drivel. Avoid at all costs!

Clint Burnham has an obsession with "sex" and "communism". Every lecture he has somehow connected the topic of marxism/communism back to sex. ENGL115W is supposed to be a class involving the study of literature within the wider cultural field, however Burnham has turned it into his own fetish of communism and sex. His lisp makes class insufferable.

He's awful. He seems pretty knowledgeable of his subject but the way he teaches isn't so easy to understand and it is really hard for 1st year students(especially those who are not major in English) to get what he teaches. I wouldn't recommend him.

Clint Burnham is a washed up writer who resents the world and puts all that resentment on his students. He gives awful advice on writing and often bashes one's work. He would teach what not to do and never tell us why or build us up to become writers. I felt like I became a worse writer in his class and sucked the inspiration out of me. Avoid him.

Burnham is obviously an intelligent person, but he has no interest in hearing others opinions/feedback or teaching in accessible ways. He refused to make his criteria clearer when I asked. He weirdly triggered by random words (like curate?). He will doc you marks if he disagrees with you. 

In private he is open to meeting but very condescending.

Clint's expectations for what he is looking for in assignments are very unclear. He will give you a confusing prompt, give you a bad mark, and THEN tell you what he was looking for in feedback. Information that would have helped in the first place. His feedback can also be rude at times and very unhelpful.

This guy LOVES to pontificate woke and often too-niche opinions in student lectures with students who likely have no idea what he is talking about. He is the kind of prof that will dock marks if he disagrees with you. He makes completely random connections between philosophy, history, psychology, etc, and talks as if everyone knows what he means.
great prof -- super funny & interesting. cares a lot about social causes and often tries to make class relevant to things that are actually happening in the world.

A man for who pedagogy is a four-letter word. Treats his students with contempt, and is only concerned with his own self-aggrandizement. Terminally obsessed with a certain faddish Slovenian philosopher who is now way past his prime.

A very strange guy. He obviously cares a lot about creative writing, just not yours. Dont feel comfortable about sharing your negative life experiences to him in your writing? Expect low grades. Also, hes pretty poor at communicating to female students. His comments are commonly belittling.
